goal: Move every piece of per-device runtime state that today lives in-memory on one Render instance — oplog+cursor seq, device tokens, OAuth provider tokens, quota/trial counters, connection records, and pending OAuth links (incl. the PKCE codeVerifier) — into durable Postgres behind the EXISTING store seams, so a redeploy no longer wipes identity/history/entitlement. Concretely: (1) flip the currently-synchronous `ConnectionsStore` interface in web/lib/connections/memoryStore.ts to async (Promise-returning) and propagate `await` through all ~17 route consumers + backfill/oauth libs; (2) add a `SupabaseConnectionsStore` mirroring the dual-backend pattern already proven in web/lib/accounts/store.ts (live when SUPABASE_URL+SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY set, in-memory otherwise) with a durable, gap-free, monotonic per-device `seq` via a per-device Postgres advisory lock (D16b) so the client is NOT forced into a 401→cursor-reset→full re-pull on deploy; (3) reconcile the connections-store device/token record with the accounts-store `devices`/`osmo_devices` map and route entitlement reads through the single `subscriptionForDevice(deviceId)` resolver (D16c), keeping `deviceByToken` as a durable accessor; (4) encrypt oauth_tokens at rest; (5) resolve SSE cross-instance delivery (events.ts is a single-instance globalThis Map) via EITHER an enforced single-instance guard OR Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY fanout (D3 — the choice is a human gate). This is the foundation ~15 other findings rest on; it must not change the wire contract in web/lib/connections/types.ts.
verifySubstrate: Green/red command run from /Users/home/Osmo/web: `npm test && npx tsc --noEmit`. `npm test` = `vitest run` over tests/**/*.test.ts (14 files, currently GREEN — confirmed memoryStore.test.ts + sse.test.ts pass 9/9). `npx tsc --noEmit` is the compile oracle that flags every un-awaited call site the instant the ConnectionsStore interface goes async (tsconfig has noEmit:true, strict:true). CAVEAT the plan requires: the in-memory tests CANNOT exercise Postgres advisory-lock seq density or the restart/no-cursor-reset property, because there is no DB in CI. Therefore the FIRST Ralph iteration must WRITE a store-agnostic contract suite (web/tests/durableStore.contract.test.ts) parameterized over a `makeStore()` factory — it runs against the in-memory impl in CI (fast, deterministic, gates the async refactor) and against a real Postgres ONLY when `OSMO_TEST_DATABASE_URL` is set (skipped otherwise). tasks.desc: Verify the Apple identity JWT in /api/account/link. humanGates.what: HG1 — Structured-output mechanism for Claude: forced tool_use with a DraftSet JSON schema vs. a JSON-instruction prompt with a tolerant parse fallback.
humanGates.why: Design/LLM-provider decision that affects prompt-cache prefix layout (D16a shared cached core), token cost, and reliability of the typed DraftSet. Not a mechanical choice — pick it (consult the claude-api skill for tool_use/JSON-mode tradeoffs) before T1 hardens the wire.

humanGates.what: HG2 — Where the deterministic floor gate's fact-consistency check runs: server (requires the device to send the S0 FactBlock/factHash in the request, changing the contract) vs. an on-device re-gate of the returned DraftSet.
humanGates.why: S0 FactBlock is computed on-device in the [KEEP] pipeline, so the server gate can only do present/distinct/length unless the contract carries the FactBlock. This is a contract-shape decision that changes the wire — must be decided, not guessed by a loop.

humanGates.what: HG3 — Retry/backoff/timeout budget: max attempts, base delay + backoff/jitter, and the client + server timeout values.
humanGates.why: Retries directly multiply Anthropic spend against the Phase-0-A circuit breaker and trade off against user-perceived latency. Give defaults, but the operator must sign off on the cost/latency envelope before it ships. Judgment + cost, not machine-verifiable.

humanGates.what: HG4 — Streaming UX + gate-reconciliation design (T7-design): how a visible token stream reconciles with a deterministic floor gate that may repair/replace the output (flicker-vs-lock), plus SSE-passthrough vs chunked-JSON transport.
humanGates.why: Streaming and a post-completion floor gate are in genuine tension — you cannot prove the gate passed until generation finishes. The UX resolution is a product/design decision that must precede the T7-impl loop.

humanGates.what: HG5 — Degraded-mode user-facing copy/labeling and the policy that autodraft SILENTLY skips a degraded draft (no saved [mock]).
humanGates.why: Per B3's honesty principle (never present mock/outage as real), the wording of the degraded chip and the silent-skip behavior is product copy + honesty policy — must be human-authored, not Ralph-generated.

humanGates.what: HG6 — Manual UI verification of the App-target wiring (not covered by swift test): confirm autodraft never saves a mock/degraded draft, the DEGRADED chip renders distinctly from the DEMO/mock chip, quota mirrors the server header, and streaming tokens appear progressively.
humanGates.why: App/Sources/App/* is built via XcodeGen (project.yml), outside Package.swift's test targets, so no Ralph loop can prove these behaviors. Verify by running the app (/run or /verify) before considering M6 done.

milestone: M7 · Phase 2 — Ed25519-signed config registry + kill-switch enforcement
goal: Replace the unsigned, unauthenticated /api/config/flags endpoint with an Ed25519-SIGNED /api/config/registry (D14d) that carries all volatile config — flags (incl. aiDrafting kill-switch), thresholds, per-task directives, and per-task model ids — signed by a DISTINCT registry key domain (separate from the entitlement signer, C8). Build the client fetch→verify→cache→offline-fallback ladder in Swift: a valid signed registry is applied and cached as last-known-good; on signature/verify failure the client falls back to last-known-good, then to app-bundled defaults (aiDrafting ON). A kill (aiDrafting=off) can therefore only be asserted by a valid signature and can never be forged or silently dropped, while availability fails safe to last-known-good. Finally make the kill-switch REAL server-side: /api/suggest must read aiDrafting from the registry and degrade BEFORE any Anthropic call, so the switch is not client-enforced only.
dependsOn: Phase 0-A — cost & key protection (server-side model allowlist consumes the registry's per-task model ids; task-typed suggest contract rewrites the same /api/suggest handler where the kill-switch check lands)
Phase 0-B — durable server state (Appendix C config_registry store; M7 ships env/file-seeded first and migrates the registry persistence to Postgres under 0-B)
Entitlement signer (web/lib/license/sign.ts + EntitlementVerifier.swift) — sibling pattern reused, not a hard prerequisite; registry uses a SEPARATE key domain

Harness code lives in web/eval/, meta-tests in web/tests/eval/ (picked up by the existing vitest include glob `tests/**/*.test.ts`), CI in a new .github/workflows/ci.yml. milestone: M9 · Phase 4 — connection lifecycle, durable job queue + out-of-request worker, and the background S4/S5 quality gate
goal: Turn every long-lived / cross-request connection operation into a durable, restart-safe, idempotent job run by an out-of-request worker (service-principal auth, quota/budget/Safety keyed to account_id per D15a), and close the four connection-lifecycle holes the audit named: (1) fire-and-forget backfills lost on restart → resumable idempotent jobs; (2) OAuth tokens captured then discarded → a refresh-on-read loop for Gmail/Slack/X so sends don't die ~1-2h after connect; (3) no inbound path after the one-shot import → Gmail history-diff, Slack Events, X DM polling; (4) blocking send with no idempotency/echo/timeout → durable send_outbox with idempotency key + optimistic local echo + bounded retry + server-side timeout; plus disconnect that actually revokes the provider token and writes a tombstone. On top of that substrate, add the background quality gate: a precomputed_draft store (D15a) whose worker runs S4 generate-2 → judge → S5 conditional repair per new inbound, idempotent and budget-accounted against the 0-A spend breaker.
dependsOn: Phase0-B-durable-state (durable Postgres for oplog/device_tokens/oauth_tokens-encrypted/connections/pending_links/event_log + cross-instance SSE fanout via LISTEN-NOTIFY — the worker and every store below sit ON this; today all state is in-memory in web/lib/connections/memoryStore.ts)

Phase0-A-cost-key-quota (per-ACCOUNT quota_counters, global Anthropic spend circuit-breaker, server-side Safety re-run — the worker's service principal reuses this exact enforcement keyed to account_id per D15a)
Phase1-draftset-floor-gate (typed DraftSet{takes} + S3 floor gate + judge task contract — the S4/S5 quality-gate handler reuses this pipeline; without it there is no DraftSet to precompute)
D0-endpoint-conventions (webhook signature verification substrate, uniform error taxonomy, disconnect-revokes-token rule — the inbound webhooks and disconnect path implement D0)

tasks.desc: Durable job_queue store + seam. New store mirroring the dual in-memory/Supabase pattern of web/lib/accounts/store.ts. Job row per D15a/Appendix-C: {id, account_id, device_id, kind: 'backfill'|'quality_gate'|'token_refresh'|'inbound_poll'|'send', platform, payload JSONB, status: 'pending'|'running'|'done'|'failed', attempts, next_run_at, cursor JSONB (resume checkpoint), last_error, created_at}. Claim via FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED (Supabase) / array shift (memory). Expose enqueue(), claimNext(), complete(), fail(withBackoff), and drainJobsForTests(). Also the precomputed_draft store keyed (account_id, threadID, lastMessageID) per D15a.

tasks.desc: Out-of-request worker runtime: a poll-claim-run-retry-complete loop with exponential backoff on fail, per-kind handler registry, and service-principal auth (attributes each job to account_id from the row; all quota/budget/Safety accounting keyed to that account_id per D15a — reuses Phase0-A enforcement, never a device Bearer token). Expose start()/stop()/drainJobsForTests().

Worker boot + single-worker guard. Register the worker loop from web/instrumentation.ts (which today only calls ensureUnipileWebhooks) behind a durable Postgres advisory lock so N instances don't each run every job — mirrors the D10 boot-registration lock design. tasks.desc: Refactor backfills from fire-and-forget to resumable idempotent JOBS. Today web/app/api/oauth/{google,slack,x}/callback, /api/connect/notify, /api/connect/rebackfill, and accounts.adoptOrphanedAccounts all `void backfill…()` in-request (lost on restart). Change them to enqueue a 'backfill' job; move backfillConnection/backfillGmail/backfillSlack/backfillX into job handlers that persist their paging cursor (Gmail pageToken, Slack next_cursor, X pagination_token, Unipile cursor) into job.cursor each page so a restart RESUMES instead of restarting. Ingest is already idempotent (dedup on platform+messageID in memoryStore.appendRows), so re-runs never duplicate.

OAuth refresh-on-read accessor. At token-store time, compute expires_at from expires_in and persist it. New freshAccessToken(accountId, platform): if within skew of expiry, refresh and re-persist the new bundle to the durable (encrypted) oauth_tokens store. Add refreshGoogleToken() + refreshSlackToken() to providers.ts (refreshXToken already exists at web/lib/oauth/providers.ts:124 but has ZERO callers). Route every provider call (send.ts, backfill handlers, inbound handlers, /api/media) through the accessor instead of the raw stored token. NOTE: default Slack user tokens do not expire (document; only refresh if token-rotation is enabled — see gate M9-G7).
tasks.note: Unit-testable with mocked fetch for the refresh round-trip; the LIVE round-trip against real Google/Slack/X needs real secrets → gate M9-G5.

tasks.desc: Inbound handlers after backfill. Gmail: history-diff job (users.history.list since last historyId, persisted in job.cursor) that appends new messages; the Pub/Sub push receiver route is the low-latency doorbell, the polling job is the fallback/truth. Slack: Events API receiver route with url_verification challenge echo + request signing-secret verification + message.im event → appendRows. X: inbound_poll job that pages /2/dm_events since the last seen dm_event id (no DM webhook on base tier). Each appends via store.appendRows and publishes a sync.dirty doorbell — same one ingest path as Unipile's webhook.

tasks.desc: Send outbox (D4). Durable send_outbox {idempotency_key PK, account_id, platform, threadID, text, status: 'pending'|'sent'|'rejected'|'failed', attempts, last_error}. Refactor /api/sync/send: read a client idempotency key from SendRequest (extend types.ts); 409 on duplicate key; insert the outbox row; RETURN the optimistic WireMessage echo IMMEDIATELY (stop blocking on the provider). Enqueue a 'send' job that performs the real provider call WITH the idempotency key, applying a server-side send timeout; provider 422 → status 'rejected' + a send.status reject doorbell; transient 5xx → backoff retry ≤ N. Replace today's inline blocking sendGmail/sendSlack/sendX/Unipile call in web/app/api/sync/send/route.ts.

tasks.desc: Disconnect token revocation + tombstone (D0). Today accounts DELETE (web/app/api/accounts/route.ts:94) only stopDrip + removeConnection — no revoke, no tombstone. Also: invalid-token detection on ANY provider call (send/backfill/inbound) marks the connection 'degraded' + publishes a connection.status degraded doorbell to prompt re-auth (D0 + D14c).

tasks.desc: Background quality-gate handler (S4/S5) + precomputed_draft (D15a). A 'quality_gate' job, enqueued when a new inbound changes a thread's lastMessageID, runs S4 generate-2 → judge → S5 conditional repair (reusing the Phase-1 DraftSet pipeline + judge task contract), then stores DraftSet + judge verdict + lineageId into precomputed_draft keyed (account_id, threadID, lastMessageID). Idempotent (same-key re-run is a no-op; a newer lastMessageID misses the key and recomputes — same rule as intel_cache). Worker runs as service principal; the 2-4× LLM multiplier is budget-accounted against the Phase0-A Anthropic spend breaker and skips when the breaker is tripped.

tasks.desc: Worker doorbells across instances. publish() in web/lib/connections/events.ts is in-process only (globalThis subscriber Set). A worker job on instance B must reach an SSE subscriber on instance A. The advisory-lock code is ralphable; the topology choice and the render.yaml/deploy change are not — and per the standing rule nothing deploys without explicit sign-off.

humanGates.what: Gmail inbound transport decision + GCP Pub/Sub topic for users.watch.
humanGates.why: Real Gmail push requires creating a Google Cloud Pub/Sub topic + push subscription bound to a public endpoint and granting gmail-api-push permission — an external paid cloud account and config. The watch-vs-history-poll design tradeoff and the topic creation must be a human; only the history-diff polling handler is ralphable.

humanGates.what: Slack Events API subscription registration + scope additions (re-consent).
humanGates.why: Enabling inbound requires editing the Slack app config dashboard (Event Subscriptions + Request URL verification) and adding scopes that force every connected user to re-consent — an external-account config change with user-facing re-auth impact, not unit-testable.

humanGates.what: X (Twitter) DM inbound tier + polling cadence / rate-limit budget.
humanGates.why: X has no DM webhook on the base tier, so inbound is polling; the cadence and whether a paid API tier is required is a paid/rate-limit decision that touches cost and X account terms — must be decided by a human before the poll job runs live.

humanGates.what: Live OAuth credential round-trip for refresh (M9-T5) and revoke (M9-T8).
humanGates.why: The refresh and revoke helpers are unit-tested with mocked fetch, but the real Google/Slack/X token refresh + revocation round-trip needs live client secrets and real connected accounts, and revocation is irreversible (kills the user's session). Verify live only under human supervision with test accounts.

humanGates.what: Sign-off on the 2-4× background-quality-gate LLM spend multiplier against the Phase0-A Anthropic budget/breaker.
humanGates.why: The S4/S5 gate multiplies Anthropic spend 2-4× per precompute (bg-quality-gate-cost-multiplier); the daily/monthly budget allocation and the precompute-eligibility policy are a money decision that must be approved and wired into the 0-A circuit-breaker, not chosen by an autonomous loop.

humanGates.what: Slack token-rotation enablement decision.
humanGates.why: Default Slack user tokens do not expire; enabling rotating tokens (so refreshSlackToken is actually exercised) is a Slack app config change with re-consent implications — a human must decide whether to enable it rather than the loop assuming a refresh path that Slack isn't configured to provide.

milestone: M11 · Cross-cutting ops, release & Phase 6 deferral
goal: Make the Osmo backend operable, releasable and honest under load: (1) one shared durable rate-limit substrate across ALL routes (D0) replacing the per-process Maps; (2) real webhook signature verification (Unipile HMAC D17b, Stripe constructEvent) with event-id dedup; (3) SSRF + streaming size-cap hardening for /api/media and avatar proxy (D5, D14c); (4) hard build-time gating of every dev/mock/reset route out of production (D10); (5) durable, editable-without-redeploy /api/health (liveness+readiness incl. DB+Anthropic reach) and /api/version incl. a min-app-build compatibility field (D10); (6) structured logging + metrics + alerting on restart/error-rate/spend/quota/SSE-subscriber-leak/webhook+backfill failure (D10, cross-cutting); (7) boot Unipile-webhook reconciliation moved behind a durable advisory lock so N instances don't each reconcile (D10 #47); (8) explicit Phase-6 deferral — the dormant static-salt E2EE Sync/ stack (SyncEngine/BlobStore/CryptoBox) disabled behind a build flag and the privacy claim scoped to single-device (D11); plus the Sparkle OTA delivery + 3rd-Ed25519-domain signing, the 3-domain secrets-rotation runbook, and server-PITR + local-DB-key-escrow DR (D16d) — the last three carrying human gates for every paid/irreversible/deploy step and honoring the standing no-auto-release rule.
dependsOn: M0B-durable-postgres-state
M0A-cost-key-protection
M0C-payments-entitlement

CRITICAL: no tests exist yet for any M11 web task (rate-limit substrate, webhook signature, media SSRF, build-gate, durable health/version, observability, boot advisory-lock) — so each Ralph loop MUST WRITE its vitest spec FIRST (tests/rateLimit.test.ts, tests/webhookSig.test.ts, tests/mediaSsrf.test.ts, tests/buildGate.test.ts, tests/health.test.ts, tests/version.test.ts, tests/observability.test.ts) and only then implement until that spec + the whole suite + build are green. OTA release / notarization / Supabase PITR / secret rotation have NO machine verify and are human-gated.

tasks.desc: Shared durable rate-limit substrate (D0): new lib/ratelimit/store.ts = Postgres/Supabase token-bucket keyed by (route-class × account|device|ip) backed by the rate_limit_buckets table (Appendix C), and lib/ratelimit/limits.ts = per-class config (enrich 10/60s, device/register, auth/request). Replace the per-process Map in enrich/person, and add limits to device/register (defeats infinite-trial minting) and auth/request. WRITE tests/rateLimit.test.ts first (bucket refill, over-limit 429, per-key isolation).
tasks.note: Needs M0B's rate_limit_buckets table + Supabase client. The register throttle also closes 0-A infinite-trials-open-register; coordinate the key shape with M0A quota-per-account.

tasks.desc: Webhook signature verification + idempotency (D0, D17b): new lib/webhooks/verify.ts with a constant-time HMAC verifier. Replace the naive `header === secret` equality in webhooks/unipile/route.ts and connect/notify/route.ts with real HMAC over the raw body (secret via header, query only as deprecated fallback per D17b) + provider-event-id dedup. In webhooks/stripe/route.ts replace the JSON.parse scaffold with stripe.webhooks.constructEvent signature verification. WRITE tests/webhookSig.test.ts first (valid sig passes, tampered body 401, replayed event-id deduped).
tasks.note: Stripe constructEvent needs the `stripe` SDK dep landed by M0C; if absent the loop stops on that item and appends it to HUMAN-GATES.md. Never-error-at-Unipile invariant must be preserved (return 200 on bad payload, 401 only on bad signature).

tasks.desc: SSRF + pre-buffer size-cap hardening for media & avatars (D5, D14c): new lib/media/ssrf.ts (https-scheme + host-allowlist check, streaming byte-count cap helper). Harden app/api/media/route.ts so 413 is enforced from streamed content-length/byte-count BEFORE fully buffering for ALL providers (today Gmail base64 and Unipile buffer the whole payload; only Slack checks content-length), extend the host allowlist beyond files.slack.com to the avatar proxy path, and make a degraded/refresh-failed BUT owned connection fall back to the 1x1 placeholder (no-store) instead of 502 (D14c). WRITE tests/mediaSsrf.test.ts first (untrusted host 400, oversize 413 before buffer, degraded-token→placeholder).

Build-time gate dev + mock routes out of production (D10): new lib/ops/prodGate.ts exporting a guard on OSMO_ENV !== 'production'. Apply to dev/emit, dev/outbox, connect/mock/complete, checkout/mock-complete, license/reset (today these gate on isLiveUnipile()/STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, which leaves them live whenever those happen to be unset). Add `OSMO_ENV: production` to render.yaml. WRITE tests/buildGate.test.ts first (each route 404s when OSMO_ENV=production, works otherwise).

Durable, redeploy-free health/version (D10): new lib/ops/health.ts (readiness pings Supabase + Anthropic reachability) and lib/ops/releaseInfo.ts. Rewrite app/api/health/route.ts to serve liveness+readiness and read the incident banner from the durable status_banner row (replacing the OSMO_STATUS env). Rewrite app/api/version/route.ts to serve from the durable release_info table (version/build/downloadURL/notes) plus a `minAppBuild` compatibility field the server can bump without redeploy. WRITE tests/health.test.ts + tests/version.test.ts first.
tasks.note: Needs M0B status_banner + release_info tables. Which build to require as minAppBuild, and WHEN to bump it (D15b app-fleet gating), is an ops judgment — the field plumbing is ralphable, the value/timing is a human gate.

tasks.desc: Observability substrate (D10, cross-cutting): new lib/ops/logger.ts (JSON structured logs with the same PII-scrub as the Phase-3 log-scrub test), lib/ops/metrics.ts (counters), lib/ops/alert.ts (fires on: process restart in instrumentation.ts, Anthropic error-rate + spend breach hooked into the M0A circuit-breaker, quota exhaustion, SSE subscriber-count leak via events.ts subscriberCount threshold, webhook + backfill failures). Wire into instrumentation.ts and lib/connections/events.ts. WRITE tests/observability.test.ts first (metric increments, leak threshold trips, scrubber redacts). The alert LIB + wiring are ralphable; the actual delivery to a real ops channel (secret webhook URL) is a human gate.
tasks.note: Spend/error-rate alerting depends on M0A's Anthropic circuit-breaker existing to observe. Alert-channel secret (OSMO_FEEDBACK_WEBHOOK / ops Slack) value = human gate.

tasks.desc: Boot webhook reconciliation under a durable advisory lock (D10 #47): move ensureUnipileWebhooks out of the per-process `ensured` flag / per-instance instrumentation.ts call into one idempotent migration guarded by a Postgres advisory lock, so N instances don't each reconcile Unipile hooks. Extend the existing tests/webhooks.test.ts to assert single-runner semantics.
tasks.note: Needs M0B Postgres for the advisory lock. tests/webhooks.test.ts already exists with a fakeClient seam to extend.

tasks.desc: Phase-6 explicit deferral — disable the dormant E2EE stack (D11): gate Sources/OsmoCore/Sync/{SyncEngine,BlobStore,CryptoBox}.swift behind a compile-time build flag (default OFF) so the static-salt CryptoBox (defaultSalt = 'osmo.static.salt.replace.in.prod', HKDF-not-Argon2id) can NEVER be enabled in a shipped build. Do NOT touch the ACTIVE Sources/OsmoCore/Backend/RealtimeSyncEngine.swift (that is the live doorbell-pull engine, unrelated). Verify with `swift build && swift test`.
tasks.note: Mechanical disable is swift-build-verifiable. The public single-device privacy-CLAIM wording is a separate human gate (legal/product).

tasks.desc: Client min-app-build enforcement (D10/D15b): the Mac app reads `minAppBuild` from /api/version (AppModel.refreshHealth/refreshFlags at ~L271/L281) and, when the local CFBundleVersion is below it, surfaces a forced-update prompt driving the existing UpdaterController.checkForUpdates(). Verify with `swift build && swift test` + a unit test around the comparison.
tasks.note: Compare logic is ralphable/testable; a wrong threshold could soft-brick the fleet, so the actual minAppBuild value shipped is the human-gated ops decision from T5.

tasks.desc: Local SQLCipher DB-key escrow + device-loss DR decision (D16d): today KeychainDBKey.loadOrCreate writes a single un-escrowed 0600 key file via LocalSecretStore — lose it and a Time-Machine-restored DB is unopenable. Make the key escrow-recoverable (e.g. iCloud Keychain item) so a restored DB file opens, and document the accepted limitation that on-device message data is NOT server-backed (device loss = local loss by design, re-ingest from iMessage/providers). Design + key-material handling = human gate; code lands after the decision.
tasks.note: D16d. Escrow mechanism, recovery UX, and 'accepted limitation' wording are product/privacy decisions touching key material — not Ralphable.

tasks.desc: Secrets rotation runbook — 3 Ed25519 domains + shared secrets (D15b, cross-cutting): author build/RUNBOOK-secrets-rotation.md covering ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GOOGLE/SLACK/X client secrets, OSMO_WEBHOOK_SECRET, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, and the THREE distinct Ed25519 domains — entitlement (OSMO_LICENSE_PRIVATE_D/PUBLIC_X, app-bundled pubkey), the Phase-2 registry signer, and the Sparkle OTA EdDSA key (SUPublicEDKey in App/Info.plist / SPARKLE_ED_KEY). Document the D15b 3-step app-bundle transition (ship both pubkeys → cut over signing → drop old) gated on the min-app-build field for the two app-bundled domains. Writing the doc is fine; EXECUTING any rotation is human-gated.
tasks.note: Doc has no machine verify → one-shot write, not a Ralph target. The rotation EXECUTION is the separate human gate below.

tasks.desc: Backup/DR — server Postgres PITR (D16 cross-cutting): enable point-in-time-recovery on the Supabase/Postgres instance and document the restore drill. Pure external-account/dashboard config; no code.
tasks.note: Supabase dashboard + possibly paid tier. External account, not Ralphable.

tasks.desc: Sparkle OTA delivery + /download wiring (D10 #69/#59): the pipeline scripts already exist (scripts/release.sh, scripts/publish-ota.sh, scripts/publish-appcast.sh) and Info.plist already carries SUFeedURL=https://leftonread.in/appcast.xml + a real SUPublicEDKey. Remaining code-side work: confirm/repair the /download landing wiring (web/app/page.tsx references) to point at the current GitHub release, and register the OTA EdDSA key as the named 3rd domain in the runbook (T11).
